name: pwf-enforcer
description: Bridges pwf's Claude Code hooks to omo's OpenCode hook system (Tier 3 advisory)

positive_triggers.input: Set up pwf hooks for OpenCode via omo
positive_triggers.reason: User wants pwf hooks in OpenCode environment

positive_triggers.input: I want hard enforcement of pwf, not soft compliance
positive_triggers.reason: User wants to upgrade pwf's soft compliance

positive_triggers.input: The agent keeps skipping phases and not updating progress.md
positive_triggers.reason: Soft compliance failure, needs hook enforcement

negative_triggers.reason: Trivial change, no hook setup needed

negative_triggers.input: I use Claude Code, pwf hooks work natively there
negative_triggers.reason: Claude Code has pwf hooks built-in, no bridge needed

negative_triggers.input: I don't use pwf at all
negative_triggers.reason: Skill only makes sense with pwf installed

behavioral_evals.scenario: User asks: 'Set up pwf hooks for OpenCode via omo'
expected_behavior: Agent verifies prerequisites: pwf installed + omo installed
Agent identifies omo hook events (session.created, tool.before.*, file.changed, session.idle, experimental.session.compacting)
Agent documents Tier 3 limitation (OpenCode cannot hard-block, only notify)
Agent writes valid hook JSON to oh-my-openagent.json
Agent tests each hook type fires (writes / edits / compaction / idle)
Agent commits project-level config for team sharing
Agent verifies progress.md gets updates (advisory firing works)
Agent recognizes this is soft compliance failure
Agent explains OpenCode Tier 3: advisory only, cannot hard-block
Agent offers alternatives: switch to Claude Code/Codex CLI for true hard enforcement, OR strengthen SKILL.md language, OR rely on agent self-discipline
Agent does NOT promise hard enforcement that OpenCode cannot deliver

notes: Tier 3 limitation documented honestly. Skill does not over-promise hard enforcement on OpenCode.
